WITN08060100
WITN08060100

SSC

12. I believe I was in the SSC during my time at Fujitsu. It was a very strange environment. We were told everything had to be fixed as quickly as possible in any way that it could. This was because any long-term outages would cost money.

13. I felt there was a huge pressure on staff to get things fixed. There was one individual in particular who placed a lot of pressure on me. There seemed to a priority of short-term fixes over long term solutions. I felt this pressure came from the top.

14. I can't remember the structure of the teams in the support services.

15. I don't recall how many people worked on the SSC. I was gradually getting to know people before I left.

16. I don't recall the different positions or roles of people on the SSC.

17. I don't recall what qualifications or experience were required to be a member of the SSC. I remember a lot of the team seemed to have been there for a number of years, having been pulled through from ICL to Fujitsu. There didn't seem to be many newer employees. I appeared to be the exception coming from the outside. In terms of my own recruitment I just went through an interview and was asked about various aspects of the system which I had worked on in the past.

18. I never finished the training which would have consisted of working in each area of the system before specializing in one.

19. I don't know how members of the SSC were briefed or instructed to carry out their work.

Page 3 of 6
